system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;

    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Wrong token type (punctuation comma)

**Error Message:**
```
wrong token type - expected Scalar, found on line 15 the punctuation token ','
```

This error occurs at line 15 of `blockMeshDict`, which is in the `vertices` section. The parser is having trouble reading the vertex coordinates.

## Root Cause

Looking at the current `blockMeshDict` carefully, the issue is in the **Block 1b definition**:

```
hex (16 17 18 19 17 16 19 18) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
```

This block definition uses vertices `(16 17 18 19)` for one face and `(17 16 19 18)` for the other face — the second face is a **reversed duplicate** of the first face (just reordered). This creates a degenerate/zero-volume block, which confuses the parser and causes the "wrong token type" error.

Furthermore, looking at the vertex numbering carefully, **all vertices 12-19 are at x=25** (none at x=0), making Blocks 1a and 1b degenerate 2D slabs. Combined with Block 1 having vertices at both x=0 and x=25, the topology is fundamentally broken.

## Recommended Solution — Clean Minimal Topology

Given that Attempts 1-4 have all failed with variations of the same topological issues, I propose a **completely different approach** with only **3 blocks** and a simplified but valid geometry:

### 1. Simplify to 3 blocks (remove the degenerate Block 1b entirely)

Remove Block 1b (`hex (16 17 18 19 17 16 19 18)`) completely. Merge its y-range (y=-75 to -50) into Block 1a by expanding Block 1a to cover the full y-range from y=-75 to y=25, divided into two zones (floor and ceiling).

### 2. Redefine vertices and blocks cleanly:

**Vertices (keep as-is, just add clarity):**
```
vertices
(
    // Block 0: Inlet straight section (x=-50 to 0, y=-25 to 25)
    (-50,  25,  25)   // 0
    (-50,  25, -25)   // 1
    (-50, -25, -25)   // 2
    (-50, -25,  25)   // 3
    (   0,  25,  25)   // 4
    (   0,  25, -25)   // 5
    (   0, -25, -25)   // 6
    (   0, -25,  25)   // 7

    // Block 1: Inner bend corner (x=0 to 25, y=-25 to 0)
    (  25,  25,  25)   // 8
    (  25,  25, -25)   // 9
    (  25, -25, -25)   // 10
    (  25, -25,  25)   // 11

    // Block 1a: Outer bend floor (x=0 to 25, y=-50 to -25)
    (   0, -50,  25)   // 12
    (   0, -50, -25)   // 13
    (  25, -50, -25)   // 14
    (  25, -50,  25)   // 15

    // Block 1b: Outer bend ceiling (x=0 to 25, y=-75 to -50)
    (   0, -75,  25)   // 16
    (   0, -75, -25)   // 17
    (  25, -75, -25)   // 18
    (  25, -75,  25)   // 19
);
```

**Blocks:**
```
blocks
(
    // Block 0: Inlet straight section
    hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Inner bend corner
    hex (4 8 9 5 7 11 10 6) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1a: Outer bend floor (y=-50 to -25)
    hex (12 16 17 13 15 19 18 14) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1b: Outer bend ceiling (y=-75 to -50)
    hex (16 17 18 19 17 16 19 18) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
)
```

**The critical fix for Block 1b:** Change the vertex ordering to `hex (16 17 18 19 16 17 19 18)` — but actually, the second face should be the **bottom face** at y=-75: `(16 17 18 19)`. So the correct definition is:
```
hex (16 17 18 19 16 17 19 18)  // WRONG — duplicates
```

**Correct Block 1b:** Since it's a flat slab at x=25 spanning y=-75 to y=-50, it needs vertices at **two different x-planes**. Add vertices at `(0, -75, ±25)` and `(25, -75, ±25)`, then define:
```
hex (16 17 18 19 16 17 19 18)  // Still wrong
```

**The real fix:** Block 1b needs vertices at two x-locations. Add vertices at `(0, -75, 25)=20`, `(0, -75, -25)=21`, `(25, -75, 25)=22`, `(25, -75, -25)=23`. Then:
- Block 1a: `hex (12 20 21 13 15 23 22 14)`
- Block 1b: `hex (16 22 23 17 19 20 21 18)`

### 3. Most Reliable Approach — Copy the Official Tutorial

The surest path forward: **Copy the exact `blockMeshDict` from `$FOAM_TUTORIALS/compressible/rhoSimpleFoam/squareBend/system/blockMeshDict`**. That file is known to work. Then, only modify the vertex coordinate values to match the user's specified geometry (inlet at x=-50, mid-bend plane at x=25, outlet depth extending to y=-75 and y=-125).

Specifically:
- Take the tutorial's block topology (typically ~10-15 blocks with proper convex geometry)
- Replace the vertex coordinate values with the user's values: set inlet at x=-50, bend corner at x=25, and extend the outlet section downward to y=-75 to y=-125
- Keep all block definitions, edge definitions, and boundary assignments identical to the tutorial
- Only the **numeric vertex positions** change; the block connectivity stays the same

### 4. Update Boundary Names Consistently

After copying the tutorial's `blockMeshDict`, ensure all `0/` field files (`U`, `T`, `p`, `k`, `epsilon`, `nut`, `alphat`) use the **exact same patch names** as defined in the copied `blockMeshDict` (likely `inlet`, `outlet`, `upperWall`, `lowerWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall`).

## Error 2: `log.rhoSimpleFoam` — Cascading error

This is a direct consequence of `blockMesh` failing. No separate action needed.

---

## Summary

1. **The Block 1b definition `hex (16 17 18 19 17 16 19 18)` is degenerate** — it has identical vertex sets for both faces, creating a zero-volume block that crashes the parser.

2. **The fundamental fix** is to either:
   - **Copy the official squareBend tutorial's `blockMeshDict`** and only scale/translate vertex coordinates to match the user's geometry, **OR**
   - **Completely redesign all blocks** so each has vertices at two distinct x-planes (no degenerate blocks)

3. **Do not attempt to fix the current blockMeshDict incrementally** — the topology is too deeply flawed. Starting fresh with a known-working tutorial file is the most efficient path.

4. After successful mesh generation, update all `0/` field files to match the boundary names from `blockMeshDict`.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': "Replace entire blockMeshDict with a corrected version based on the official squareBend tutorial but adapted to the user's specified geometry; Define vertices properly: place inlet at x=-50 spanning y=-25 to 25 and z=-25 to 25, place mid-bend plane at x=25 spanning y=-25 to 0 and z=-25 to 25, and place outlet section extending down to y=-75 and z=-25 to 25; Create non-degenerate hex blocks connecting these regions with proper vertex ordering; Use 10 cells in each direction initially; Set scale to 0.001; Define boundary patches as inlet, outlet, upperWall, lowerWall, leftWall, rightWall matching the tutorial topology; Remove any degenerate or zero-volume blocks"}, {'file': 'system/fvSchemes', 'changes': 'Keep existing scheme definitions unchanged since they appear reasonable'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1500. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 0.71.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
